goal: Build a small Node CSV<->JSON converter as a CLI. Provide a pure-function core module exporting csvToJson(text) (parse CSV with a header row into an array of objects) and jsonToCsv(rows) (serialize an array of objects back to CSV), handling standard CSV quoting rules correctly. Add a CLI entry that reads an input file (or stdin) plus a --to csv|json flag and writes the converted result to stdout. Include node:test unit tests for the core functions. Test command: npm test.

sprints.title: Project scaffolding and core module skeleton
sprints.description: Initialize a Node package (package.json with type=module, test script set to 'node --test'). Create the directory layout: a core module file (e.g., src/csv.js) exporting stub csvToJson(text) and jsonToCsv(rows) as pure functions, and a CLI entry file (e.g., bin/cli.js) with a shebang. Establish the public API signatures and module boundaries so subsequent sprints have stable contracts to build against. No parsing logic yet — just exports and wiring that import/run cleanly.

sprints.title: Implement CSV parsing and serialization with quoting rules
sprints.description: Flesh out the pure-function core. csvToJson(text): parse a CSV with a header row into an array of objects, correctly handling RFC 4180 quoting — quoted fields, escaped double-quotes (""), commas and newlines inside quotes, and trailing/empty lines. jsonToCsv(rows): derive columns from object keys (union across rows), serialize back to CSV, quoting fields only when needed (containing comma, quote, or newline) and escaping embedded quotes. Ensure round-trip fidelity for standard cases. Keep both functions side-effect free.

sprints.title: Build the CLI entry point
sprints.description: Implement the CLI on top of the core module. Accept an input file path argument or read from stdin when none is given. Parse a required --to csv|json flag to select the conversion direction (csv input -> JSON output, or json input -> CSV output). Read input, invoke the appropriate core function, and write the result to stdout. Handle basic error cases (missing/invalid --to flag, unreadable file, malformed input) with a nonzero exit code and a clear stderr message.

sprints.title: Unit tests for core functions
sprints.description: Write node:test (node --test) unit tests covering the core functions. For csvToJson: basic header+rows, quoted fields, embedded commas/newlines, escaped quotes, empty/whitespace edge cases. For jsonToCsv: correct header derivation, quoting only when required, quote escaping, and heterogeneous key sets. Include at least one round-trip test (csvToJson then jsonToCsv) to lock in fidelity. Ensure 'npm test' runs green.
